cmd.openOnRight: Open on the right (secondary side bar)
ext.description: AI-assisted code generation for data and AI teams. Multi-provider (HubGPU/OpenAI/Anthropic), governed Skills, project profile, quality gates, Ed25519 license, observability, and in-network MCP.
capabilities.untrustedWorkspaces: Some FORGE settings define commands it runs locally (warehouse connections, MCP catalog, run/test commands) or NETWORK DESTINATIONS it sends data to (gateway, RAG embeddings, Langfuse). In an untrusted workspace these settings are ignored until you trust the folder — preventing a malicious repository from injecting a command or redirecting egress to an arbitrary host.
config.gateway.url.md: URL of the **in-network gateway** that validates the license, proxies inference, and emits traces. Empty = local mode (client-side validation only; see `forge.license.mode`).
config.license.mode.md: `gateway`: authoritative server-side validation (recommended in production). `local`: Ed25519 verification on the client only (dev/PoC).
config.skills.managedDir.md: Directory of the **admin-managed** skills catalog. Empty = uses the catalog bundled with the extension.
config.skills.retrievalThreshold.md: Above this number of enabled skills, discovery switches to selecting top-K by relevance (RF-037).
config.skills.topK.md: Number of skills injected in discovery when relevance retrieval is active.
config.skills.templates.md: In Project Mode, materializes as a forge-file the **deterministic scaffold** (`.tmpl`) declared in the frontmatter of activated skills — outside the LLM, as gap-fill (never overwrites what the LLM generated). Proposals inherit the gate. Turn off to not materialize skill scaffolds.
config.egress.allowExternal.md: Allow egress to the public internet. **Default: false** (deny-by-default, RNF-014). Do not change without security approval.
config.egress.allowedHosts.md: Allowed in-network hosts (provider, gateway, Langfuse, MCP, license). Any destination outside this list is blocked and logged.
config.egress.trustInNetwork.md: Automatically allow **in-network LAN hosts** (`.internal`, `.local`, private IPs 10.x/192.168.x/172.16-31.x) without listing them in `allowedHosts`. **Default: true** (backward-compatible — convenient on a closed corporate network). Set **false** for **defense in depth**: with `false`, only **loopback** (`localhost`/`127.x`/`::1`) is allowed automatically and any LAN host must be explicitly in `forge.egress.allowedHosts` — prevents a setting (e.g. `forge.rag.embeddings.url`, `forge.gateway.url`) from redirecting egress to an arbitrary internal host (defense against intra-network exfiltration/SSRF). This setting is ignored in an untrusted workspace.
config.mcp.catalog.md: Catalog of **admin-managed** MCP servers (managed setting). Each item follows the `McpServerEntry` schema.
config.validation.gateBlocksApply.md: When a skill defines a validator as a *gate*, a validation failure blocks the **Apply** of the diff (RF-039).
config.gate.definitionOfDone.md: **Definition of done** gate (P2) in **Project Mode**: when the project was generated in full but is missing a **dependency manifest** (requirements.txt / pyproject.toml), **any test** (test_*.py / tests/), or a **README with "## How to run"**, it blocks the **Apply** of all files (block + warning, no auto-repair). Deterministic and conservative — only flags a real absence. Turn off (`false`) for projects where tests/README do not apply. Respects the master `forge.validation.gateBlocksApply`.
config.gate.blockUnverifiedContract.md: Admin policy (**Project Mode**, **Python** projects): while the **cross-file contract has NOT been verified** (mypy did not run — no venv/mypy; or the whole gate could not run), it **BLOCKS with no escape** Apply all, "Force blocked", **and the Apply of each project card**. The way out is **Prepare environment** (creates the venv) and **Re-verify contract** (the gate installs mypy in the venv and re-checks the SAME proposals, without regenerating). Scope `machine`: a value in the repository's `.vscode/settings.json` is **ignored** (the workspace cannot turn off the policy); it deliberately does **not** respect the master `forge.validation.gateBlocksApply` (which is workspace-overridable). On a network without PyPI, provide an internal mirror (`pip config set global.index-url …`) before enabling — without installable mypy, Python projects stay blocked. Default `false` (explicit confirmation, classic behavior).
config.gate.security.md: **Security** gate (P2) in **Project Mode**: runs **bandit** (SAST) over generated **Python** and a built-in **pure-TS SAST** over generated **TypeScript/JavaScript** — catches hardcoded password/secret, dynamic code execution (`eval`/`new Function`), shell-command injection (`exec`/`execSync` with a dynamic command), weak crypto, etc. (AST/pattern analysis; does **not** run the code). `conservative` (default): only the high-precision code-execution / shell-injection classes block Apply (bandit HIGH+HIGH for Python; the pure-TS SAST for TS/JS — very few false positives); the rest is advisory. `advisory`: never blocks. `off`: does not run. Tool missing → advisory (fail-open); bandit is installed best-effort into the workspace venv. Respects the master `forge.validation.gateBlocksApply`.
config.gate.deadImports.md: **Dead imports** gate (P2) in **Project Mode**: runs **ruff** (rule `F401`) over the generated Python project and lists unused imports as **advisories** — it **never** blocks Apply. AST analysis (does **not** run the code); `--isolated` ignores any discovered `ruff.toml`/`pyproject`; only `F401` is selected. ruff missing → advisory (fail-open); if it is in the workspace venv, it is installed best-effort. Python only. Default `true`.
config.gate.security.enum.0: Only high-precision code-execution / shell-injection findings block Apply (bandit HIGH+HIGH for Python; the pure-TS SAST for TS/JS); the rest is advisory (default).
config.gate.security.enum.1: Runs the security scan and shows the findings, but NEVER blocks Apply.
config.gate.security.enum.2: Does not run the security gate.
config.gate.sql.md: Deterministic **SQL engine** (data): analyzes every `.sql` proposal **in-process** (installing nothing) — statement classification, ~16 **anti-pattern** rules with declared confidence (SELECT *, cartesian product, `NOT IN` + NULL, non-sargable predicate, dead CTE, LIMIT in a dbt model…), and **schema validation** against the dbt project's `target/manifest.json` when present (a nonexistent table/column becomes a finding with a suggestion). dbt models with Jinja (`{{ ref() }}`) are analyzed natively. `conservative` (default): only **security** findings block Apply; the rest is advisory. Respects the master `forge.validation.gateBlocksApply`.
config.gate.sql.enum.0: Only SQL SECURITY findings (DELETE/UPDATE without WHERE, DROP/TRUNCATE, cartesian product) block Apply; anti-patterns and schema (dbt) are advisory (default).
config.gate.sql.enum.1: Analyzes and shows the findings on the proposal card, but NEVER blocks Apply.
config.gate.sql.enum.2: Does not run the SQL engine.
config.warehouse.connections.md: **Warehouse** connections (Wave 3 — data): FORGE runs SQL through the **CLIs you already use** (traditional path) or via **MCP** from the catalog. Each item: `{ id, kind: oracle|postgres|bigquery|duckdb|s3|oci-os, connect, readonly?, tool?, walletDir?, schemas?, mcp? }`. Examples — Oracle 19c/26ai/Exadata: `{ "id": "dw", "kind": "oracle", "connect": "user@DWPROD" }` (SQLcl/sqlplus; password in SecretStorage, prompted on first use); **ADW**: add `walletDir` (becomes TNS_ADMIN); PostgreSQL: `connect` = psql URI; BigQuery: `connect` = GCP project (gcloud auth), `schemas` = datasets; S3/OCI Object Storage: `kind: s3|oci-os` (aws/oci CLI). **Engine governance**: SELECT runs directly; writes require `readonly:false` ON the connection + per-execution confirmation; DROP/TRUNCATE **never** run. Samples are capped and masked (LGPD) before any display.
config.warehouse.default.md: Connection used by the data commands when no `id` is given (empty = the first in the list).
config.warehouse.rowCap.md: Maximum **rows** displayed from a SELECT (sample — LGPD: the chat never loads a dataset). 1–500.
config.warehouse.timeoutSeconds.md: Timeout (s) of each warehouse execution via CLI.
config.project.reconcileDependencies.md: **Dependency** reconciliation (P4) in **Project Mode**: after the gate, checks whether the generated `requirements.txt` declares the packages the code actually **imports** and adds the **missing** ones to the manifest proposal (idempotent; preserves pins/comments; **conservative** — never adds an ambiguous package, filters out stdlib and local modules). Complements the definition-of-done gate (which ensures the manifest **exists**; this one ensures it is **correct**). `pyproject.toml`-only projects are excluded (editing TOML is fragile). Turn off (`false`) to not auto-edit the manifest proposal.
config.rag.enabled.md: Enables codebase context retrieval (RAG) via semantic/lexical search (RF-041).
config.rag.embeddings.url.md: Base of the **in-network** OpenAI-compatible embeddings endpoint (ends in `/v1`). **Do not include `/embeddings`** — that suffix is added by the client. E.g. `https://hub-gpus.claro.com.br/embed06b/v1`. Empty = **lexical** retrieval (BM25), no embeddings (RF-079).
config.rag.embeddings.dimensions.md: Vector density (MRL/Matryoshka). **0 = model default** (1024 for Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B). Changing the density requires reindexing the codebase (FORGE: Reindex codebase).
config.rag.maxChunks.md: Maximum number of codebase chunks injected into the context per generation. With the dynamic context budget (the model's real window), it is worth injecting more references; the excess that does not fit the budget is cut automatically.
config.rag.maxFileSizeKb.md: Files larger than this (KB) are skipped during indexing.
config.rag.include.md: File globs included in codebase indexing.
config.rag.exclude.md: Globs of files/folders excluded from indexing. Includes `.env` by default. Matching is by path substring (after removing `**/` and `/**`); avoid patterns that match legitimate code (e.g. `secret` would exclude `credential_service.py`).
config.telemetry.enabled.md: Opt-in product telemetry (distinct from the admin's observability via gateway/Langfuse).
config.diagnostics.enabled.md: **Local diagnostics** log (P3): writes a structured, always-**redacted** NDJSON (secrets/PII masked) to `globalStorage/logs`, plus an in-memory buffer. Feeds the **FORGE: Export diagnostics** command (a bundle to attach to a bug report). Always-on and **100% local** — distinct from Langfuse observability, which is opt-in and leaves the machine. Turn off (`false`) to write nothing to disk.
config.identity.requireEmail.md: Requires the dev to provide their **email** during initial setup (becomes the trace `userId` in Langfuse). Use `true` when licenses are shared/generic. Even with `false`, the email is requested if the license `subject` is not a valid email.
config.outputLanguage.md: **Generation output language**: the language the model must respond in (chat, explanations, generated docs). `auto` follows the **VS Code UI language** (en → English; anything else → pt-BR, the product default). UI language and generation language are independent — you can use the UI in English and generate in pt-BR. Does not affect code identifiers.
config.outputLanguage.enum.0: Follow the VS Code UI language (en → English; otherwise pt-BR)
config.provider.maxOutput.md: Override of the **output token cap** per generation. **0 = uses the model catalog** (e.g. 32k for gpt-oss-120b). Set manually only if the HubGPU/vLLM gateway accepts a different cap. Very high values may be rejected by the gateway (HTTP 400).
config.provider.maxContextWindow.md: Context window **actually served by the gateway** (vLLM's `--max-model-len`). **0 = uses the model capacity** from the catalog (e.g. 128k for gpt-oss-120b). Set this value if HubGPU serves the model with a window **smaller** than the nominal one, so the context budget does not overflow (HTTP 400).
config.run.enabled.md: Allows **running** the generated code from the panel (Run button / command). Execution is local; deny-by-default egress still applies.
config.env.timeoutSeconds.md: Maximum time (s) for **Prepare environment** (venv + pip install) — heavy installs on a cold cache easily exceed the normal execution timeout.
config.run.commands.md: Overrides the run commands per extension. E.g. `{ ".py": "python {file}" }`. `{file}` is the file path. Merged over the defaults.
config.test.enabled.md: Enables **TDD Mode** and the **Run tests** command.
config.test.command.md: Test suite command, run at the workspace root (e.g. `pytest -q`, `pytest tests/`, `npm test`).
config.test.autoInstall.md: When **pytest is not installed** in the environment, installs it automatically in the venv before running (without asking). Off = asks first.
config.search.server.md: ID of the **internal search MCP server** (present in `forge.mcp.catalog`). When set, enables **Search (internal network)** in the context menu — governed search (egress/approval/audit), **no public internet**. Empty = search disabled.
config.search.tool.md: Name of the search MCP tool exposed by `forge.search.server`.
config.search.queryArg.md: Name of the argument that receives the search text in the MCP tool.
config.project.managedProfile.md: Path to an **admin-managed project profile** (markdown — the organization's standards/conventions). When set, it is injected into every prompt as the **lowest-precedence** layer: the workspace's `.forge/project.md` still has the final say. Empty = no admin layer.
config.ocr.tesseractPath.md: Path to the `tesseract` executable for **OCR on pasting a screenshot**. Empty = **auto-detection** (PATH + default install locations, including per-user in `%LOCALAPPDATA%`). Set it if tesseract is in a non-standard/**portable** location — so you need no admin and no install in `Program Files`. Without tesseract, OCR only shows a warning; the rest of FORGE works normally.
config.ocr.tessdataPath.md: `tessdata` folder with the OCR languages (e.g. `eng.traineddata`, `por.traineddata`). Empty = uses tesseract's default `tessdata`. Point it to a **user-writable** folder to add `por` **without admin**.
config.observability.mode.md: Destination of **observability** (generation + workflow: skill, apply/discard, gate, execution, tests, review). `gateway` is the **governed relay** — events go through the gateway authenticated by the session token, and the **Langfuse secretKey never exists on the client** (closes the governance gap). `direct` keeps the legacy mode (keys on the client). If unset, the legacy `forge.observability.langfuse.enabled=true` is equivalent to `direct`.
config.observability.mode.enum.1: Client → Langfuse directly (the secretKey lives on the client). Personal/PoC mode.
config.observability.mode.enum.2: Client → GATEWAY → Langfuse (the gateway holds the secretKey server-side; the Admin governs sampling/capture). Recommended corporate mode.
config.observability.currency.md: Currency label used in the estimated cost (just a label — e.g. `R$`, `US$`). See `forge.observability.pricing`.
config.observability.budget.md: Session spend ceiling (FinOps, in the `forge.observability.currency` unit). Warns at ~80% and blocks new generations once reached. `0` = no ceiling. Only applies when `forge.observability.pricing` is set (no price ⇒ no cost ⇒ no ceiling). This is a client-side deterrent; the authoritative token/day budget is signed into the license and enforced by the gateway.
config.observability.pricing.md: **Per-model prices** for the estimated cost (FinOps) shown in the Langfuse trace. Key = substring of the `modelId` (the most specific wins); value = `{ "input": <price per 1M input tokens>, "output": <per 1M output> }`. E.g. `{ "claude-sonnet": { "input": 18, "output": 90 }, "gpt-oss": { "input": 0, "output": 0 } }`. **Empty (default) = no cost is emitted** — FORGE does not fabricate prices (HubGPU is self-hosted; cost is the organization's decision).
config.observability.langfuse.enabled.md: **(Legacy)** Enables **direct** observability (client → Langfuse). Prefer `forge.observability.mode` (`direct`/`gateway`). Kept for backward compatibility: with this `true` and no `mode` set, the effective mode is `direct`. The secretKey stays on the client — for Admin governance use `gateway`.
config.observability.langfuse.baseUrl.md: Langfuse base (e.g. `https://cloud.langfuse.com`, `https://us.cloud.langfuse.com`, or your self-hosted instance). The host must be in `forge.egress.allowedHosts`.
Langfuse project public key (`pk-lf-…`). The secret key stays in SecretStorage (command `FORGE: Configure observability (Langfuse)`).
config.observability.langfuse.sampleRate: Fraction of generations sampled (0 to 1). 1.0 = all.
config.observability.langfuse.capture.md: How much of the prompt/output to capture: `full` (raw), `masked` (redacts secrets/PII — default), or `metadata-only` (no content).
